stories.headline: Avalanche trade Nichushkin to Columbus Blue Jackets, receiving three draft picks
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche traded forward Valeri Nichushkin to the Columbus Blue Jackets on Thursday, receiving a second-round pick in the 2026 NHL Draft, a third-round pick in 2027 and a fifth-round pick in 2028 in return. The 31-year-old had 49 points in 72 regular-season games this past season and four points in 12 Stanley Cup Playoff games.

Nichushkin has four years remaining on the eight-year contract he signed with Colorado in July 2022. He was a key contributor to the Avalanche's 2022 Stanley Cup championship run.

The move is the third forward trade Colorado general manager Joe Sakic has made in the past two weeks, following the deals that sent Ross Colton and Jack Drury to the Nashville Predators. The flurry of trades signals a significant reshaping of the Avalanche's roster and the accumulation of draft capital heading into an apparent rebuild phase.

stories.headline: CU Denver to purchase downtown Independence Plaza tower for $29.75 million, an 80% discount from 2007 price
stories.summary:
The University of Colorado Denver is set to acquire Independence Plaza, a 25-story, 567,000-square-foot office tower at 1050 17th St. in downtown Denver, for $29.75 million. The building's current owner paid $144.5 million for it in 2007, making the sale to CU a discount of nearly 80 percent. The Board of Regents voted Monday to approve the purchase, which is expected to close Friday.

The Denver Downtown Development Authority also approved $4.5 million to help fund a 30,000-square-foot regional innovation and entrepreneurship hub at the site, led by CU Denver and Denver Economic Development and Opportunity. The university said it would use the building to expand its downtown presence, move departments out of aging campus buildings and create opportunities for students to connect with nearby employers.

The acquisition reflects the broader trend of downtown Denver office buildings selling at steep discounts following the pandemic-era decline in office occupancy. Independence Plaza is currently only 21 percent occupied. CU Denver noted that constructing a new 72,000-square-foot engineering building on the Auraria Campus had been shelved because it would have cost $90 million, making the discounted tower a far more cost-effective option.

stories.headline: Colorado voter guide: Primary election one week away with governor, Senate and statewide offices on June 30 ballot
stories.summary:
Colorado voters head to the polls on June 30 for the 2026 primary election, with races for governor, U.S. Senate, attorney general and secretary of state among the major contests. Registered voters who have not yet mailed their ballots must use a drop box, vote at a voter center or vote in person on Election Day, with polling places open from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m.

The Democratic gubernatorial primary pits U.S. Sen. Michael Bennet against Attorney General Phil Weiser, while Republican candidates include State Sen. Barbara Kirkmeyer, State Rep. Scott Bottoms and Victor Marx. The U.S. Senate Democratic primary features State Sen. Julie Gonzales against incumbent Sen. John Hickenlooper, with State Sen. Mark Baisley running unopposed on the Republican side.

The attorney general race on the Democratic side includes Secretary of State Jena Griswold, Michael Dougherty, Hetal Doshi and David Seligman, while Republicans Michael Allen and David Willson are competing for that nomination. Because Griswold is running for attorney general, both parties also have candidates competing for the secretary of state seat she would vacate.

stories.headline: Update: Montbello car break-in victim confronted suspects before fatal shooting, Denver police say
stories.summary:
Denver police have released new details indicating that the man fatally shot early Wednesday in the Montbello neighborhood was killed after he confronted a group of suspects who were breaking into cars in the 15000 block of East Olmstead Place. The victim, a vehicle owner, came outside to confront the suspects and was shot during the confrontation.

Police have not made any arrests and are still working to develop suspect information. Investigators are asking anyone with information to contact Metro Denver Crime Stoppers at (720) 913-7867.

The new details establish the sequence of events for the first time, clarifying that the shooting was directly linked to an active car break-in. The case remains open as detectives seek to identify and locate those responsible.

stories.headline: Colorado regulators approve reduced $2 million fines for oil and gas operators in falsified data scandal
stories.summary:
The Colorado Energy and Carbon Management Commission approved settlements Wednesday with six oil and gas operators who unknowingly filed falsified environmental data at 344 Front Range drill sites. The companies — Bonanza Creek, Kerr-McGee Corp., Noble Energy, Crestone Peak Resources, Extraction Oil and Gas and Highpoint Energy — will collectively pay approximately $2 million in fines and contributions to public projects, far below the $11 million maximum the ECMC's fine schedule would have allowed.

The falsified data was submitted by two consulting firms: Eagle Environmental Consulting of Wheat Ridge and Tasman Geosciences of Broomfield, whose employees altered laboratory test results for soil, groundwater and organic contaminants — including benzene and arsenic — between 2021 and the summer of 2024. Eagle Environmental notified clients including Chevron and Civitas Resources of the fraud in July 2024, who then alerted the ECMC.

The commission said an assessment of the affected sites found no additional risk to public health, though some required revised remediation plans. Community members and at least one commissioner criticized the penalty reductions as insufficient, with residents questioning how regulators could maintain credibility when self-reported data from operators proved untrustworthy. The fines were reduced in part because operators agreed to settle rather than contest penalties at a litigated hearing.

stories.headline: Update: Overnight rains slow Dry Creek Fire in Garfield County; evacuation orders lifted
stories.summary:
Overnight rainfall provided relief to firefighting crews battling the Dry Creek Fire in Garfield County near Rifle, preventing further growth and allowing officials to lift evacuation orders Thursday morning. The fire has burned approximately 316 acres since igniting Tuesday afternoon south of Rifle.

A home and an outbuilding were destroyed in the initial outbreak of the blaze, though no injuries have been reported. The Upper Colorado River Interagency Fire Management Unit Type 3 team took over management of the fire from local agencies Wednesday morning.

Crew priorities for Thursday shifted to securing the fire perimeter and extinguishing hot spots within the burn area. Hand crews remain on the ground and one aircraft is on standby. The cause of the fire is still under investigation.

stories.headline: RTD board votes to reinstate BroncosRide bus service after five-year suspension
stories.summary:
The Regional Transportation District's board voted 10-5 this week to bring back BroncosRide bus service, which shuttles fans from Park-n-Ride lots across metro Denver to Broncos home games at Empower Field. The service was suspended before the 2020-21 season due to bus driver shortages and concerns about transit equity.

The reinstated service will cost approximately $1.6 million, though RTD staff estimated fare revenues of nearly $498,000 could offset a portion of that expense. Agency officials said the program is expected to boost RTD's lagging overall ridership and improve the appeal of public transit, even as the agency navigates a broader budget crisis.

RTD plans to announce detailed logistics before the Broncos' Aug. 21 preseason home game against the Green Bay Packers. The service will operate approximately 92 buses from about 18 locations around metro Denver, including stops near Denver International Airport, Highlands Ranch Town Center, Broomfield, Longmont, Littleton and Parker.

stories.headline: Rockies showing new grit at season's midpoint, young players fueling turnaround from last-place depths
stories.summary:
The Colorado Rockies are displaying a markedly different attitude through the first half of the 2026 season, driven by young players including rookie outfielder Cole Carrigg, Jake McCarthy and TJ Rumfield. The team remains in last place but has shed the hopeless character that nearly led to one of baseball's all-time worst records a year ago.

Recent highlights include a walk-off three-run triple by McCarthy to defeat the Boston Red Sox at Coors Field on June 22. Carrigg, a speedy rookie, has become a symbol of the club's new hustle-first mentality under a new front office.

The shift in team culture reflects deliberate personnel and development decisions by Colorado's new front office, which is focusing on building through youth rather than short-term fixes. While the Rockies are not yet a contending team, observers note the change in competitive energy as a meaningful step forward.

stories.headline: Evergreen businesses damaged after overnight flash flooding along State Highway 74
stories.summary:
Flash floods struck the Evergreen community overnight Wednesday into Thursday, damaging businesses along State Highway 74. The Evergreen community rallied in the aftermath, though the full scope of damages was still being assessed.

Flash flooding is a recurring hazard in mountain communities along Colorado's Front Range foothills during summer storm season, particularly in areas with steep terrain and limited drainage capacity. State Highway 74 is the primary commercial corridor through Evergreen.

No further details on injuries or the number of affected businesses were immediately available. Residents and local officials were responding to the damage as cleanup efforts began Thursday.

stories.headline: Xcel Energy proposes 11.4% natural gas rate increase; Colorado PUC opens July public comment period
stories.summary:
The Colorado Public Utilities Commission is inviting public comment in July on several matters, including Xcel Energy's proposal to raise residential natural gas bills by an average of 11.4 percent, or $7.59 per month. Small business gas bills would increase by approximately 13 percent, or $36.47 per month, under the proposal filed by Xcel on Dec. 29, 2025.

Xcel says the rate increase would fund safety, integrity and modernization investments in its natural gas infrastructure. The utility is also proposing a $5 million shareholder-funded contribution to its Gas Affordability Program to help offset the impact on lower-income customers.

A remote public comment hearing on the gas rate case is scheduled for July 28 from 4 to 6 p.m. via Zoom. A final decision from the commission is expected in the third quarter of 2026. The PUC's outreach follows new state legislation, signed in June 2026, requiring the commission to enhance public access and include summaries of public comments in its decisions beginning Sept. 1, 2026.

stories.headline: Fourth of July fireworks guide: Displays scheduled across metro Denver for 2026 Independence Day weekend
stories.summary:
Communities across the Denver metro area have scheduled fireworks and entertainment events for the Fourth of July holiday weekend, with July 4 falling on a Saturday in 2026. Events are planned in Arvada, Aurora, Brighton, Broomfield, Berthoud and numerous other cities, with most fireworks displays starting between 9 and 9:30 p.m.

Colorado law prohibits all personal fireworks that explode or leave the ground, including bottle rockets, firecrackers, Roman candles and M-80s. Violations can result in fines exceeding $1,000 and possible jail time. If fire restrictions or a Red Flag Day are in effect, no fireworks of any kind are permitted.

This year's celebration also marks the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States. Residents are advised to check county-by-county fire restriction updates at coemergency.com before attending or planning any fireworks-related activities, and to leave pets, alcohol, drones and weapons at home.

stories.headline: Kansas City bar owner opens Denver cocktail bar in former Goldspot Brewing space in Regis neighborhood
stories.summary:
A new cocktail bar called Something Good Denver is planned to debut in August in a space previously occupied by Goldspot Brewing in Denver's Regis neighborhood. The bar is being opened by a Kansas City-based bar owner expanding into the Denver market.

The opening adds to activity in the Regis neighborhood's hospitality scene and follows a recent wave of both openings and closures in Denver's bar and restaurant sector. Goldspot Brewing previously occupied the location before closing.

Further details on the ownership group and specific address were not immediately available, but the bar is expected to open in August 2026.

weather.notable: Nearly 7 degrees above the average high for this time of year.

joke.joke: I'm reading a book about gravity. It's impossible to put down.

weekendEvents.title: Usher and Chris Brown: The R&B Tour
weekendEvents.description: Two of R&B's biggest stars share the stage at Empower Field at Mile High for one of the summer's most anticipated stadium shows. Expect career-spanning hits and elaborate choreography from both performers.
weekendEvents.location: Empower Field at Mile High, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Denver Cherry Blossom Festival (Sakura Matsuri)
weekendEvents.description: The 52nd annual Cherry Blossom Festival at Sakura Square celebrates Japanese heritage and culture with live entertainment including taiko drumming, food and drink, marketplace exhibits, traditional Japanese carpentry demonstrations, and Sogetsu Ikebana flower-arranging displays. Admission is free.
weekendEvents.time: 11 a.m.–6 p.m. Saturday; 11 a.m.–5 p.m. Sunday

weekendEvents.title: Denver PrideFest
weekendEvents.description: Denver PrideFest marks its 52nd anniversary with a weekend of events across downtown Denver, culminating in the Vizzy Denver Pride Parade on Sunday. The festival is welcoming and inclusive for all attendees.

weekendEvents.title: Grand Slam Paint Jam
weekendEvents.description: A new outdoor mural festival from art collective Vision88, featuring more than 20 artists painting live on Lawrence Street. Live music accompanies the event, and proceeds benefit Art for Eyes, which provides prosthetic eyes for children.

weekendEvents.title: Denver Dog Fair
weekendEvents.description: The Denver Dog Fair takes over the National Western Complex with dog adoptions, agility demonstrations, doggy derby runs, contests, and a puppy kissing booth.

weekendEvents.title: Troutfest Colorado
weekendEvents.description: A festival celebrating conservation and the outdoors at Coors Field, with exhibitors, fly tying and casting clinics, and live music. Open to anglers, outdoor enthusiasts, and families.

weekendEvents.title: Denver Pride 5K
weekendEvents.description: A 5K run and walk at Cheesman Park benefiting The Center on Colfax, an LGBTQ+ resource center. Both running and walking are welcome, and participants are encouraged to wear festive attire.

weekendEvents.title: Cruise Room Pride Burlesque Show
weekendEvents.description: Kick off Denver Pride weekend with a Pride-themed burlesque performance by Denver's Hard Candy Dance Entertainment at the historic Cruise Room inside the Oxford Hotel.
weekendEvents.location: Cruise Room at Oxford Hotel, 1600 17th St., Denver

weekendEvents.title: The Bright Light Social Hour at Levitt Pavilion
weekendEvents.description: The Austin-based psych-rock outfit delivers a high-energy outdoor performance blending rock, funk, and electronic influences at the free Levitt Pavilion concert series.

weekendEvents.title: Bodies: The Science Within
weekendEvents.description: This national touring exhibition makes its U.S. debut in Denver at the Denver Pavilions, taking visitors on a journey through real human specimens to explore the intricate systems that sustain life. The engagement runs through June 28.

weekendEvents.title: Donkey Derby Days
weekendEvents.description: The 95th annual Donkey Derby Days in Cripple Creek features a parade, live music, and donkey derby races celebrating the town's mining history, hosted by the Two Mile High Club.
weekendEvents.time: 2–8 p.m. Friday; 7 a.m.–8 p.m. Saturday; 7 a.m.–5 p.m. Sunday

weekendEvents.title: Blues from the Top Music Festival
weekendEvents.description: A multi-day blues music festival set in Winter Park featuring live performances across the weekend.

topStory.headline: Iran Strikes Singapore-Flagged Vessel in Strait of Hormuz, UN Halts Ship Evacuation Plan
topStory.summary:
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a Singapore-flagged commercial cargo vessel with a drone in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday evening, a U.S. official confirmed to CBS News and the Associated Press. The ship's bridge was damaged after it was hit on its starboard side off the coast of Dahit, Oman, according to the U.K. Maritime Trade Operations Centre, which reported no casualties or environmental damage. The vessel was not part of an ongoing UN-coordinated evacuation effort for ships stranded in the Persian Gulf.

Following the strike, the United Nations' International Maritime Organization suspended its large-scale evacuation plan for stranded vessels, citing the need to reconfirm that safety guarantees remain in place. IMO Secretary-General Arsenio Dominguez said the halt was necessary "in order to reconfirm that the necessary safety guarantees continue to be in place," adding that seafarer safety "remains paramount." The IMO had launched the evacuation effort earlier in the week, offering two transit routes through the strait — one through Iranian waters and one along the Omani coast.

The attack came amid a deepening dispute over which route ships may use and whether Iran can impose transit fees. The U.S. and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding last week under which Iran agreed to use its "best efforts" to ensure toll-free safe passage through the strait for 60 days, a deal that had briefly revived ship traffic — from six vessels per week to 70 on Tuesday alone. However, Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority warned Thursday that any vessel using routes not designated by Tehran would not be covered by safe passage guarantees or insurance. Iran has also not ruled out imposing transit tolls once the 60-day window expires.

Diplomatically, the broader U.S.-Iran negotiations remain unsettled on multiple fronts. President Trump claimed this week that Iran would purchase American wheat, corn, and soybeans using released sanctioned funds, but Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf publicly denied the claim, writing on social media that "the only crop we're harvesting is what you planted: decades of mistrust." Meanwhile, International Atomic Energy Agency chief Rafael Grossi said Friday that while an interim accord grants UN inspectors access to Iran, talks over what to do with Tehran's uranium stockpile have "barely initiated" and a "very strong" verification system must be established as soon as practicable. Secretary of State Marco Rubio separately affirmed that the U.S. and Gulf allies would ensure no fees are charged on ships transiting the strait, though Thursday's strike and the IMO pause illustrate the fragility of that commitment.
